_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- identify and document where your organisation's data comes from and how it is shared
- assess current data systems in the organisation and the technology they use
- determine the data architecture the organisation needs to achieve organisational objectives
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- building relationships with parts of the organisation that protect access to data
- working with all areas of the organisation to ensure the data architecture works for everyone and any change is accepted
- relating data architecture to strategic objectives or important user needs to gain investment
reference_document_element_description: Design and document the enterprise data architecture, including platform topology, data domain structure, integration patterns, and data lifecycle policies.
comments: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-003 (DA, cosine: 0.610), DG-K-001 (DG, cosine: 0.577).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.8/10 (raw 0.575). Shared vocabulary: [architecture, data, document]. DDaT-only terms [accepted, access, achieve, areas, assess, building] vs WIDAI-only terms [design, domain, enterprise, integration, lifecycle, patterns] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.847 (P=0.824, R=0.872). R exceeds P by 0.048 — the WIDAI KSA's 19 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 96 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (95.1%). "You can:
- identify and document where your organisation's data comes from and h" and "Design and document the enterprise data architecture, including platform topolog" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.566 ≈ STS 0.575 (gap 0.009). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [architecture, data, document] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.9%. Shared tokens: [architecture, data, document]. DDaT-only: [accepted, access, achieve, areas, assess, building]. WIDAI-only: [design, domain, enterprise, integration, lifecycle, patterns].
